questions.question: What is the binary representation of 7?
What happens to the register when the 8th ball passes through?
questions.expected_answer: The register goes from 7 (0111) to 8 (1000), flipping bit 8 from left to right
hints: The transition from 7 to 8 flips the highest bit
This flip triggers the overflow indicator

questions.question: If 15 blue balls are run through, what is the final state of the OV gear bit?
Trace the register and OV bit states for balls 1-8
questions.expected_answer: Ball 1: A=1, OV=left; Ball 2: A=2, OV=left; Ball 3: A=3, OV=left; Ball 4: A=4, OV=left; Ball 5: A=5, OV=left; Ball 6: A=6, OV=left; Ball 7: A=7, OV=left; Ball 8: A=8, OV=right (overflow triggered)
hints: Register counts up normally for balls 1-7
Ball 8 flips bit 8, triggering the overflow latch
